hook: AI gave me a 6-paragraph essay when I needed a yes or a no.
caption:
AI gave me a 6-paragraph essay when I needed a yes or a no.

You asked: "Should I take the meeting?"

Generic AI gave you: a balanced exploration of the pros, the cons, the philosophical nature of professional relationships, and three follow-up questions to consider.

You needed: "No. You already said you're overcommitted this quarter. It's not a warm lead."

The problem isn't the AI. It's that the AI doesn't know you default to action over analysis. It doesn't know you've already done the thinking and just need a push in a direction. It doesn't know that when you ask a yes/no question, you mean it.

So it hedges. It covers its bases. It gives you the answer that works for everyone, which means it works for no one.

Your thinking style is a real variable in how useful AI actually is to you. Some people want all the nuance. Some people want the bottom line. Most AI just... guesses.

BootFile stops the guessing.

When your AI profile knows you're a decisive, action-first thinker — the responses change. Shorter. More direct. Less "on the other hand."

Same AI. Completely different output.
